SEARCH
Package app.service.impl.anomalies

Class QuantumAnomaliesDetectionService<T extends TimeSeriesRow>

java.lang.Object
app.service.impl.anomalies.QuantumAnomaliesDetectionService<T>
Type Parameters:
T - The underlying time-series data row type under evaluation, extending TimeSeriesRow.
All Implemented Interfaces:
AnomaliesDetectionService<T>
Direct Known Subclasses:
QuantumDataRecordAnomaliesDetectionService, QuantumFinancialIndicatorsAnomaliesDetectionService
public abstract class QuantumAnomaliesDetectionService<T extends TimeSeriesRow> extends Object implements AnomaliesDetectionService<T>
Quantum-inspired anomaly detection service.

The public API is intentionally identical to the classical implementation: trainIsolationForest(List, int) still receives historical generic records and returns a Smile IsolationForest; searchForAnomaly(IsolationForest, List, double) still receives that dto, the target records and the UI threshold.

The difference is the representation used internally. Before fitting/scoring, each structural time-series row (e.g., OHLCV pricing or technical indicators) is encoded into a simulated multi-qubit quantum feature map:

  1. Variables are standardized against the training distribution.
  2. Each standardized feature is angle-encoded into a qubit rotation.
  3. A small variational-like circuit is simulated with Ry rotations, controlled-phase entanglement and a second mixing layer.
  4. The computational-basis measurement probabilities are used as the feature vector for Isolation Forest.

This is a local simulator, not a call to quantum hardware. It is useful when the project must stay dependency-free and compatible with the existing controller/factory, while still using quantum-style angle encoding, superposition, entanglement phases and interference before the isolation step.

  • Method Details

    • trainIsolationForest

      public smile.anomaly.IsolationForest trainIsolationForest(List<T> data, int treesNumber)
      Trains an Isolation Forest over simulated quantum measurement probabilities instead of raw dimensional values.
      Specified by:
      trainIsolationForest in interface AnomaliesDetectionService<T extends TimeSeriesRow>
      Parameters:
      data - historical generic records used as normal baseline
      treesNumber - number of trees to build during the anomaly detection process
      Returns:
      trained IsolationForest over quantum-encoded records

    • searchForAnomaly

      public List<AnomalyResult<T>> searchForAnomaly(smile.anomaly.IsolationForest isolationForest, List<T> data, double threshold)
      Scores target records through the same quantum feature map used during training.

      The method returns one AnomalyResult per input record, preserving the current GUI behavior where the threshold is drawn as a chart marker and the full score series is displayed.

      Specified by:
      searchForAnomaly in interface AnomaliesDetectionService<T extends TimeSeriesRow>
      Parameters:
      isolationForest - dto returned by trainIsolationForest(List, int)
      data - target records to score
      threshold - UI threshold; validated to keep API semantics, but not used to remove chart points
      Returns:
      scored records with feature contribution percentages

    • parseData

      protected abstract double[][] parseData(List<T> data)
      Transforms a generic collection list of financial domain records into a primitive 2D matrix structure.
      Parameters:
      data - The working List containing generic time-series instances.
      Returns:
      A primitive 2D double matrix tracking dimensional mapped values.
